content:
type: DRAFT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: image_to_base64() method + normalize_image_bytes/get_image_mimetype helpers
code: def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize raw or already base64-encoded image bytes.

    Returns a tuple of (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) where
    encoded_image is guaranteed to be base64-encoded, and
    decoded_for_guessing is the raw bytes suitable for MIME type detection.
    """
    try:
        decoded_for_guessing = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
        encoded_image = image
    except Exception:
        encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image)
        decoded_for_guessing = image
    return encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing


class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    ...

    def get_image_mimetype(self, decoded_image: bytes | None = None) -> str | None:
        """Lazily compute and cache the image mimetype.

        Args:
            decoded_image: optional raw (non-base64) bytes to guess from, to
                avoid re-decoding when the caller already has them on hand.
        """
        if self.image_mimetype:
            return self.image_mimetype
        if decoded_image is None:
            if not self.image:
                return None
            decoded_image = base64.b64decode(self.image)
        guess = filetype.guess(decoded_image)
        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing)

        return self
note: Proposed merge: my normalize_image_bytes() handles base64 detection/encoding, your get_image_mimetype() handles lazy mimetype compute+cache. image_to_base64 calls both. Let me know your exact get_image_mimetype signature/body if different so we can converge verbatim.

def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize raw or already base64-encoded image bytes.

    Returns a tuple of (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) where
    encoded_image is guaranteed to be base64-encoded, and
    decoded_for_guessing is the raw (non-base64) bytes suitable for MIME
    type detection via filetype.guess().
    """
    try:
        decoded_for_guessing = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
        encoded_image = image
    except Exception:
        encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image)
        decoded_for_guessing = image
    return encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing

                return None

        guess = filetype.guess(decoded_image)
        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
